name: The First National Bank Of Assumption
description_short: Community bank chartered in 1900 in Assumption, IL, offering checking, savings, loans, and online banking to local residents with personalized service.
description_long:
The First National Bank of Assumption was established in 1900 by A.H. Corzine, C.C. Corzine, and August Cazalet. Unlike many financial institutions that closed during the Great Depression or were acquired by larger banks, First National Bank of Assumption has maintained continuous operation for over 120 years and remains independently focused on serving its community.

The bank offers traditional retail banking services including checking accounts, savings accounts, and various loan products. They provide competitive rates and personalized loan structuring for mortgages, auto loans, and personal loans. The institution has invested in digital banking capabilities, including e-statements, online check viewing, Quicken and QuickBooks Direct Connect compatibility, mobile banking, and upcoming mobile deposit functionality.

First National Bank of Assumption distinguishes itself as a genuine hometown community bank with deep local roots and stability. The small team—three full-time and one part-time employee—emphasizes personal service and relationship banking in an era dominated by large national institutions. Leadership includes President & Loan Officer Mike Corzine, maintaining family continuity from the bank's founding.

This bank is best suited for customers who value local ownership, personalized service, and community connection over extensive branch networks. The small staff size and limited hours (Thursday and Saturday close at noon) may present accessibility challenges for some customers. Prospective clients should verify current service offerings and compare loan rates competitively, as a small community bank may have limited product breadth compared to regional or national alternatives. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Home loans with competitive rates and personalized terms
Auto loans with competitive rates and personalized terms
Personal loans for purchases and dream vacations
Quicken and QuickBooks Direct Connect capability
pros: Over 120 years of continuous operation demonstrating stability and community commitment since 1900
Personalized loan structuring with competitive rates and low costs tailored to individual needs
Modern digital banking features including e-statements, online check viewing, and upcoming mobile deposit
Direct integration with Quicken and QuickBooks for business accounting convenience
Small, accessible team with named decision-makers including President Mike Corzine available for relationship banking
Drive-up service available during extended hours (8am-4pm/5pm most weekdays) for customer convenience
Genuine independent community bank without acquisition by larger institutions
cons: Very limited staffing (3 full-time, 1 part-time employee) may result in slower service during peak times
Reduced lobby hours on Thursday and Saturday (closes at noon) and Friday afternoon closure (12-3:30pm) limit accessibility
Likely limited product breadth and loan options compared to regional or national banks
Single location in Assumption, IL with no branch network for customers needing multi-location access
Mobile deposit feature listed as 'coming soon' indicates incomplete digital service offerings
best_for: Assumption, IL residents and local business owners seeking relationship-based community banking
Small business owners needing QuickBooks integration and personalized loan structuring
Customers prioritizing local ownership and long-term stability over large institution convenience
Individuals seeking personalized service and direct access to bank leadership and decision-makers
diagnosis: First National Bank of Assumption is ideal for Assumption, IL residents and small business owners who value personalized service, local ownership, and community connection over nationwide branch networks. The main caveat is the extremely limited staffing and hours, plus single-location availability—customers needing frequent access, multiple locations, or extensive product options should compare with regional alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
